sources.description: Longitudinal health events for each citizen, recorded at clinical or coaching touchpoints over time.

columns.EventType: Type of health event: 'routine check-up', 'lifestyle coaching session', 'preventive screening', 'follow-up assessment', 'emergency visit', 'specialist consultation'. Emergency visits and specialist consultations are strong indicators of trajectory deviation.
columns.PhysicalActivityIndex: Numeric index (0-100) measuring physical activity level at this event. Declining trends signal sedentary drift.
columns.SleepQualityIndex: Numeric index (0-100) measuring sleep quality. Drops correlate with stress, shift-work disruption, or health issues.
columns.EnvironmentalExposureLevel: Numeric index (0-100) of environmental risk exposure (pollution, noise, hazardous conditions). Rising values indicate worsening living/working environment.
columns.Timestamp: ISO-8601 datetime of the event. Irregular spacing may indicate avoidance or crisis periods.

sources.description: GPS location pings capturing citizen mobility patterns. Dense local clusters indicate routine; outlier cities indicate travel. Declining location diversity or reduced mobility radius may signal withdrawal.

columns.user_id: 8-char alphanumeric citizen identifier (join key, maps to CitizenID in status)
columns.timestamp: ISO-8601 datetime of the location ping

sources.description: Static demographic profile for each citizen. Provides baseline context: age (via birth_year), occupation, residence, and travel habits.

columns.birth_year: Year of birth (used to compute age)
columns.job: Current occupation or employment status (Student, Retired, Office Clerk, etc.)
columns.residence: Nested object with city, lat, lng of primary residence
columns._travel_per_year: (Lev1 only) Expected number of trips per year
columns._travel_distance_min: (Lev1 only) Minimum travel distance in km
columns._travel_distance_max: (Lev1 only) Maximum travel distance in km

sources.description: Rich narrative descriptions of each citizen's lifestyle, personality, health behaviors, mobility patterns, and social habits. Sections headed '## CITIZENID - Name' contain qualitative information crucial for understanding behavioral deviations that numeric data alone cannot capture. Key signals include: social withdrawal, appetite changes, sleep disruption mentions, skipped medical follow-ups, and declining activity narratives.
